---
title: "Religion in New Zealand"
date: "2021-OCT-12"
output:
  ioslides_presentation:
    mainfont: roboto
    incremental: yes
    transition: 0
    smaller: no
    widescreen: no
    selfcontained: yes
    css: extra.css
  beamer_presentation:
    incremental: yes
bibliography: bibliography.bib
editor_options:
  markdown:
    wrap: 72
---




```{r global_options, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  collapse = TRUE,
  echo = FALSE,
  fig.width = 16,
  fig.height = 9
)
#results="hide",
#fig.width= 8,
#fig.height=,
#fig.fullwidth = TRUE)

# packages
library("tidyverse") # data wrangling 
library("here") # setting directories
library("ggplot2") # graph 
library("ggthemes") # themes
library("table1") # tables
library("lme4") # alluvial graphs
library("ggeffects") # alluvial graphs
library("patchwork")
library("ggplot2")
library("lubridate")


#easystats::easystats_update()
library("easystats")


```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# See: https://rstudio-pubs-static.s3.amazonaws.com/27777_55697c3a476640caa0ad2099fe914ae5.html#/
# setup
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE #,
#  layout = "l-body-outset" #,
 # fig.width= 12,
 # fig.height= 10,
#  collapse =TRUE,
#  R.options = list(width = 60)
)

```

```{r cache=TRUE, echo = FALSE}
# import data
df <- readRDS(here::here("data", "df"))
# filter years measured
df <- dplyr::filter(df, YearMeasured == 1)

# filter current wave
w19 <- df %>% dplyr::filter(Wave == 2019)
```

```{r cache = TRUE, echo = FALSE}
ord_dates_class <- c(
  "Baseline", 
  "PreCOVID", 
  "JanFeb", 
  "EarlyMarch", 
  "Lockdown",
  "PostLockdown")

# 3665 days after 30 June 2009 Saturday 13 July 2019
# 3842 days after 30 June 2009 Monday 6 January 2020
# 3895 days after 30 June 2009 Friday 28 February 2020
# 3921 days after 30 June 2009 WED MAR 25th
# 3954 days after 30 June 2009 Monday 27 April 2020
# 3672 days after June 30 2009 Saturday 20 July 2019
```


```{r cache = TRUE, echo = FALSE}
cv <- df %>%
  dplyr::filter(YearMeasured == 1) %>%
  dplyr::filter(Wave == 2018 | Wave == 2019) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Id) %>% 
  filter(n() > 1) %>%
  dplyr::filter(n() != 0) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup(Id) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Covid_Timeline =
                    as.factor(ifelse(
                      TSCORE %in% 3896:3921, # feb 29 - march 25th
                      "EarlyMarch",
                      ifelse(
                        TSCORE %in% 3922:3954,
                        "Lockdown", #march 26- Mon 27 April 2020
                        ifelse(
                          TSCORE > 3954, # after april 27th 20202
                          "PostLockdown",
                          ifelse(
                            TSCORE %in% 3842:3895, # jan 6 to feb 28
                            "JanFeb",
                          ifelse(
                            TSCORE %in% 3665:3841 &
                              Wave == 2019,
                            "PreCOVID",
                            "Baseline"  # 3672 TSCORE or  20 July 2019
                          )
                        )
                      )
                    ) 
                 ) ))%>%
  dplyr::mutate(PrePostCovid =
                  ifelse(
                    TSCORE > 3895, "PostLockdown",
                    ifelse(
                      TSCORE < 3895 &
                        Wave == 2019,
                      "PreCOVID",
                      "Baseline"
                    )
                  ))%>% 
  dplyr::group_by(PrePostCovid) %>% 
  filter(n() > 1) %>%
  ungroup() %>% # There is overlap between waves, we only want repeated measures 
  dplyr::select(
    TSCORE,
    WSCORE,
    Wave,
    Partner,
    Employed,
    Sat.Government,
    NZ.Business.Conditions,
    NZ.Economic.Situation,
    PrePostCovid,
    Rel.Conflict,
    Rel.Satisfaction,
    Hours.SocialMedia,
    Hours.Exercise,
    HoursCharity,
    Hours.Internet,
    Hours.News,
    Hours.Family,
    Hours.Friends,
    Hours.Housework,
    Hours.Children,
    Hours.Religious,
    Hours.Work,
    Hours.Commute,
    Hours.TV,
    Hours.CompGames,
    Hours.Community,
    Family.Time,
    Community.Time,
    Friends.Time,
    Family.Money,
    Friends.Money,
    Community.Money,
    years,
    Id,
    Parent,
    SWB.SatPWI02, # your health
    Hours.Work,
    Hours.Exercise,
    LIFEMEANING,
    KESSLER6,
    KESSLER6sum,
    Age,
    Male,
    Relid,
    Pol.Orient,
    Urban,
    GenCohort,
    Edu,
    YearMeasured,
    Covid_Timeline,
    HLTH.SleepHours,
    HLTH.Fatigue,
    LIFESAT,
    PWI,
    EthnicCats,
    NZdep,
    Partner,
    Pol.Orient,
    Urban,
    Euro,
    Religious,
    NZdep,
    WSCORE,
    Religion.Church,
    Emp.JobSecure,
    Emp.JobSat,
    Emp.JobValued,
    Alcohol.Intensity,
    Alcohol.Frequency,
    Your.Future.Security
  ) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Id) %>% filter(n() > 1) %>%
  dplyr::filter(n() != 0) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup(Id) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Urban = (Urban),
    Euro = (Euro),
    Religious = as.factor(Religious),
    Your_Health = (SWB.SatPWI02),
    Your_Future_Security = Your.Future.Security,
    Covid_Timeline = as.factor(Covid_Timeline)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    WSCORE = as.factor(WSCORE),
    #EduC = scale(as.numeric(Edu), scale = F, center = TRUE),
    Sat.Government = scale(Sat.Government),
   # AgeDecadeC = scale(Age, scale = F, center = T) / 10,
    Hours.Work10 = as.integer(Hours.Work) / 10,
    Hours.Sleep = as.integer(HLTH.SleepHours),
    Hours.Exercise = as.integer(Hours.Exercise),
    Fatigue = as.integer(HLTH.Fatigue),
    K6sum = as.integer(KESSLER6 * 6),
    LIFEMEANING_S = scale(LIFEMEANING),
    NZdepS = scale(NZdep),
    PolOrientS = scale(Pol.Orient, scale = T, center = T),
    Conservative = Pol.Orient,
    logSM = log(Hours.SocialMedia + 1),
    logINT = log(Hours.Internet + 1),
    logEX = log(Hours.Exercise + 1),
    logWK= log(Hours.Work + 1),
    logCT = log(Hours.Commute + 1),
    KESSLER6sum = as.integer(KESSLER6sum),
    Alcohol.Intensity_S = scale(Alcohol.Intensity),
    Alcohol.Frequency_S = scale(Alcohol.Frequency),
    Hours.Work = as.integer(Hours.Work),
    Hours.Exercise = as.integer(Hours.Exercise),
    yearS = years - min(years)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Covid_Timeline = forcats::fct_relevel(Covid_Timeline, ord_dates_class))
```

```{r cache = TRUE, echo = FALSE}
# Subset Employed
cvE <- cv%>%
  dplyr::filter(Employed == 1)

# Subset Partner
cvP <- cv%>%
  dplyr::filter(Partner == 1)

# Subset Religious
cvR <- cv%>%
  dplyr::filter(Relid != 0)
```

```{r cache = TRUE, echo = FALSE}
cv <- cbind(cv,
            demean(
              cv,
              select = c(
                "KESSLER6sum",
                "logEX",
                "logSM",
                "logINT",
                "logWK",
                "logCT",
                "Hours.Sleep",
                "Hours.Work10",
                "Hours.Exercise",
                "Sat.Government",
                "Hours.Children",
                "Hours.Internet",
                "Hours.Friends",
                "Hours.Family",
                "Hours.Religious",
                "Hours.Community",
                "Rel.Satisfaction",
                "Rel.Conflict",
                "Family.Time",
                "Family.Money",
                "Friends.Time",
                "Friends.Money",
                "Community.Time",
                "Community.Money",
                "Fatigue",
                "Alcohol.Intensity",
                "Alcohol.Frequency",
                "Emp.JobSecure",
                "Friends.Time",
                "Family.Time",
                "Community.Time",
                "Hours.SocialMedia",
                "Hours.Internet",
                "Your_Future_Security",
                "LIFEMEANING"
              ),
              group = "Id"
            )) 



# Demean employed
# cf <- dplyr::filter(df, Wave == 2019)%>% filter(YearMeasured ==1)
# length(unique(cf$Id))

# test that we only have repeated measures
# test <-cv %>%
#   group_by(Id)%>%
#   count()%>%
#   ungroup() 
```

```{r cache = TRUE}
# covid timeline
# 3665 days after 30 June 2009 Saturday 13 July 2019
# 3842 days after 30 June 2009 Monday 6 January 2020
# 3895 days after 30 June 2009 Friday 28 February 2020
# 3921 days after 30 June 2009 WED MAR 25th
library(lubridate)
library(tidyverse)


carep <- cv %>%
  dplyr::mutate(timeline = make_date(year = 2009, month = 6, day = 30) + TSCORE) %>%
  count(day = floor_date(timeline, "day")) %>%
dplyr::mutate(condition = ifelse(
    day < "2019-06-20",
    "Baseline",
    ifelse(
      day >= "2019-06-20" &
        day < "2020-01-06",
      "PreCOVID",
      ifelse(
        day >= "2020-01-06" & day < "2020-02-29",
        "Jan_Feb",
        ifelse(
          day >= "2020-02-29" & day < "2020-03-25",
          "EarlyMarch",
          ifelse(
            day >= "2020-03-25" &
              day < "2020-04-28",
            "Lockdown",
            "Postlockdown"
            )))))) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(condition = forcats::fct_relevel(condition, ord_dates_class)) %>%
  arrange(day)

# get COVID date
dates_vline0<- as.Date("2018-06-03")
dates_vline1<- as.Date("2019-06-20")
dates_vline2<- as.Date("2020-01-06")
dates_vline3<- as.Date("2020-02-28")
# get data of change of baseline
dates_vline4<- as.Date("2020-03-25")
dates_vline5 <- as.Date("2020-04-27")
dates_vline6 <- as.Date("2020-10-15")
# for line in a graph

# for line in a graph
#
dates_vline10 <- which(carep$day %in% dates_vline0)
dates_vline1b <- which(carep$day %in% dates_vline1)
dates_vline2b <- which(carep$day %in% dates_vline2)
dates_vline3b <- which(carep$day %in% dates_vline3)
dates_vline4b <- which(carep$day %in% dates_vline4)
dates_vline5b <- which(carep$day %in% dates_vline5)
dates_vline6b <- which(carep$day %in% dates_vline6)


tline <- ggplot(carep, aes(day, n)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = condition)) + scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b/%Y", limits = c(as.Date("2018-06-10"), as.Date("2020-10-15")))  + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1000))  +
 geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(carep$day[dates_vline10]),
             col = "black",
             linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(carep$day[dates_vline1b]),
             col = "red",
             linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(carep$day[dates_vline2b]),
             col = "red") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(carep$day[dates_vline3b]),
             col = "red",
             linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(carep$day[dates_vline4b]),
             col = "red",
             linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(carep$day[dates_vline5b]),
             col = "red",
             linetype = "dashed") + 
  xlab("NZAVS Waves 10 and 11 daily counts: repeated measures") + ylab("Count of Responses")  + theme_classic() +   scale_fill_viridis_d() + theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
    legend.title = element_text(color = "Black", size = 8)
  ) +
  annotate(
    "rect",
    xmin = as.Date("2018-01-03"),
    xmax = dates_vline1,
    ymin = 0,
    ymax = 1000,
    alpha = .1,
    fill = "darkblue"
  )  +
  annotate(
    "rect",
    xmin = dates_vline1,
    xmax = dates_vline6,
    ymin = 0,
    ymax = 1000,
    alpha = .001
  ) +
  annotate(
    geom = "curve",
    x = as.Date("2018-07-10"),
    y = 750,
    xend = as.Date("2019-10-15"),
    yend = 800,
    curvature = -.2,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(2, "mm"))
  ) +
  annotate(
    geom = "curve",
    x = as.Date("2018-07-10"),
    y = 750,
    xend = as.Date("2020-01-30"),
    yend = 850,
    curvature = -.2,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(2, "mm"))
  ) +
  annotate(
    geom = "curve",
    x = as.Date("2018-07-10"),
    y = 750,
    xend = as.Date("2020-03-10"),
    yend = 875,
    curvature = -.2,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(2, "mm"))
  ) +
  annotate(
    geom = "curve",
    x = as.Date("2018-07-10"),
    y = 750,
    xend = as.Date("2020-04-10"),
    yend = 900,
    curvature = -.2,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(2, "mm"))
  ) +
  annotate(
    geom = "curve",
    x = as.Date("2018-07-10"),
    y = 750,
    xend = as.Date("2020-06-10"),
    yend = 925,
    curvature = -.2,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(2, "mm"))
  ) +
  annotate("text",
           x = as.Date("2018-07-01"),
           y = 755,
           label = "0") +  annotate("text",
           x = as.Date("2019-10-25"),
           y = 800,
           label = "1") + 
   annotate("text",
           x = as.Date("2020-02-10"),
           y = 850,
           label = "2") + 
  annotate("text",
           x = as.Date("2020-03-15"),
           y = 875,
           label = "3") +
  annotate("text",
           x = as.Date("2020-04-20"),
           y = 900,
           label = "4") +
  annotate("text",
           x = as.Date("2020-06-10"),
           y = 925,
           label = "5")
```




## The New Zealand Attitudes and Values Study

-   Planned 20-year longitudinal study, currently in its 12$^{th}$ year.
-   Sample frame drawn randomly from NZ Electoral Roll.
-   Postal questionnaire (coverage; retention \~ 80%)
-   Focus on personality, social attitudes, values, religion,
    meaning-making, perfectionism, virtues, habits, employment,
    experiences of discrimination, physical and psychological health,
    and environmental attitudes ...
-   Current sample contains `r  length(unique(w19$Id))` unique people, which is $>$ 1% of
    the NZ adult population.
<!-- -   Here, *N* = `r  length(unique(w19$Id))` longitudinal participants who responded -->
<!--     to **both** the 2018/19 and 2019/20 NZAVS waves. -->


## Questionaire

```{r questions, layout="l-body-outset", fig.cap = "NZAVS questionnaire"}
knitr::include_graphics(here::here("figs", "questions.png"))
```

## Religious Change


```{r  poprelchange, fig.cap= "Clock-like Decline in Proportion of Christian Affiliation since 1966 at about 1% per year", cache = TRUE, code_folding=TRUE}
# Show decline in religion at the Society Level, using census data
# religious decline in new Zealand

# import census data
declineUSE <-
  as.data.frame(read.csv(here::here("data", "declineUSE.csv")))
decline <- declineUSE %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Christians = as.numeric(Christians),
    Population = as.numeric(Population),
    PercentagePopulation = as.numeric(PercentagePopulation)
  )

library(ggplot2)
pr <-
  ggplot(decline, aes(y = PercentagePopulation, x = Years)) + theme_bw() +
  geom_smooth(method = "glm") +
  geom_point(
    stat = "identity",
    colour = "red",
    fill = "white",
    size = 2,
    stroke = 1
  ) + labs(x = "Time", y = "Percentage Christians") + labs(title = "Aggregate proportional decline in Christian affiliation: New Zealand years 1966-2018")  + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_classic()
#plot
pr 
```

## Timeline 

```{r bigtimeline, cache= TRUE,  fig.cap = "Daily counts of data entry for the New Zealand Attitudes and Values Study (2009-2020). Red vertical lines indicate major events that affected large segments of the population. In addition to these major society-level events, countless *natural experiments* have been occurring at the individual level. Measuring people before, during, and after such exposures enables powerful inferences about the functional consequences of religion and spirituality simultaneously at both social and individual levels."}


datrep <- df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(timeline = make_date(year = 2009, month = 6, day = 30) + TSCORE) %>%
  count(day = floor_date(timeline, "day")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Year = factor(ifelse(
    day < "2010-01-01",
    2009,
    ifelse(
      day < "2011-01-01",
      2010,
      ifelse(
        day < "2012-01-01",
        2011,
        ifelse(
          day < "2013-01-01",
          2012,
          ifelse(
            day < "2014-01-01",
            2013,
            ifelse(
              day < "2015-01-01",
              2014,
              ifelse(
                day < "2016-01-01",
                2015,
                ifelse(
                  day < "2017-01-01",
                  2016,
                  ifelse(
                    day < "2018-01-01",
                    2017,
                    ifelse(day < "2019-01-01", 2018,
                           ifelse(day < "2020-01-01", 2019, 2020))
                  )
                )
              )
            )
          )
        )
      )
    )
  ))) %>%
  arrange(day)



# create date lines
ceq <- as.Date("2011-02-22")
keq <- as.Date("2016-11-14")
ter <- as.Date("2019-03-15")
cov <- as.Date("2020-02-28")

# get number for dates
ceqD <- which(datrep$day %in% ceq)
keqD <- which(datrep$day %in% keq)
terD <- which(datrep$day %in% ter)
covD <- which(datrep$day %in% cov)


# create dates

d1 <- as.numeric(datrep$day[ceqD])
d2 <- as.numeric(datrep$day[keqD])
d3 <- as.numeric(datrep$day[terD])
d4 <- as.numeric(datrep$day[covD])

# create break points
cuts <-
  data.frame(
    Big_Events = c(
      "Christchurch Earthquake",
      "Kaikoura Earthquake",
      "Mosque Attacks",
      "NZ First COVID-19 Case"
    ),
    vals = c(d1, d2, d3, d4),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )


ggplot(datrep, aes(day, n)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = Year)) + scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b/%Y")  +
  geom_vline(
    mapping = aes(xintercept = vals,
                  colour = Big_Events),
    data = cuts,
    linetype = "dashed",
    show.legend = TRUE
  )  +
  xlab("NZAVS daily counts") + ylab("Count of Responses") + ggtitle("NZAVS Daily Counts")  + theme_classic()  +  scale_fill_viridis_d()
```


```{r  msm, cache = TRUE, include = TRUE, eval=FALSE}
# Using markov models, show that change within people as they age differs from the society-wide trend

#create dataframe     
dat  <- df %>%
  filter(YearMeasured == 1 )%>% # select people who were measured at least twice
  dplyr::select(Wave,Age,Wave,Religious1,Id, Relid, years)%>%
  dplyr::mutate(yearW  = as.numeric(Wave)) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>% filter(n() > 2)%>%
  filter(n() !=0)%>%
  ungroup(Id)%>%
  arrange(Id,yearW)

#length(unique(dat$Id))


### MSN ANALYIS BELOW ###
# commenting out model to save time (which I've saved in a file, below)
# initial matrix for transitions

### MSN ANALYIS BELOW ###

library(msm)
Q0 <- rbind(c(.9,.1),
            c(.1,.9))


# Crude initial transitions 
crudeinR <- msm::crudeinits.msm(Religious1 ~ yearW, Id, data=dat, qmatrix=Q0)

# model
# gh.mod.mnm  <- msm(Religious1 ~ yearW, Id,
#                    data=dat,
#                    covariates =  ~ Age,
#                    qmatrix = crudeinR,
#                    ematrix = rbind(c(.1,.1),c(.1,.1)),
#                    est.initprobs = TRUE)
```


```{r  msm2, cache = TRUE, include = TRUE, eval=FALSE}
gh.mod.mnm <- readRDS(here::here("_posts", "religious_simpsons_paradox", "models", "mnm-simpson-paradox-religious-change.rds"))

#probability matrix
msm::pmatrix.msm(gh.mod.mnm) # The probability matrix is the same
```



```{r  hmmchange, cache = TRUE, fig.cap="Hidden Markov Model reveals religious change within people differs from the society wide-aggregate trend. As people age, they are more likely to affiliate and less likely to disaffiliate. The population gap comes from much greater disaffiliation among young people than affiliation.", include = FALSE, code_folding=TRUE}
#, fig.cap="Hidden markov model reveals religious change within people differs from the society wide-aggregate trend. As people age, they are more likely to affiliate and less likely to disaffiliate. The population gap comes from much greater disaffiliation among young people than affiliation.
# Religious Affiliation (Innit Pr = .61);  Disaffiliation (Innit Pr = .39)
# THIS GETS INITIAL PROBABILITIES
#print(gh.mod.mnm)

# note to self, this code can be improved
### CREATE PLOT  ###  CONVERSION AGE PLOT
## 20 year olds

library(msm)

gh.mod.mnm <- readRDS(here::here("_posts", "religious_simpsons_paradox", "models", "mnm-simpson-paradox-religious-change.rds"))


p20 <-
  pmatrix.msm(gh.mod.mnm, covariates = list(Age = 20), ci = "normal")
p20.Convert <- p20$estimates[1, 2]
p20.Convert.L <- p20$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p20.Convert.U <- p20$U[1, 2]
con.20 <- c(20, p20.Convert, p20.Convert.L, p20.Convert.U)
## 30 year olds
p30 <-
  pmatrix.msm(gh.mod.mnm, covariates = list(Age = 30), ci = "normal")
p30.Convert <- p30$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p30.Convert.L <- p30$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p30.Convert.U <- p30$U[1, 2]
#combine
con.30 <- c(30, p30.Convert, p30.Convert.L, p30.Convert.U)
## 40 year old
p40 <-
  pmatrix.msm(gh.mod.mnm, covariates = list(Age = 40), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p40.Convert <- p40$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p40.Convert.L <- p40$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p40.Convert.U <- p40$U[1, 2]
con.40 <- c(40, p40.Convert, p40.Convert.L, p40.Convert.U)
## 50 year old
p50 <-
  pmatrix.msm(gh.mod.mnm, covariates = list(Age = 50), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p50.Convert <- p50$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p50.Convert.L <- p50$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p50.Convert.U <- p50$U[1, 2]
con.50 <- c(50, p50.Convert, p50.Convert.L, p50.Convert.U)
## 60 year olds
p60 <-
  pmatrix.msm(gh.mod.mnm, covariates = list(Age = 60), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p60.Convert <- p60$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p60.Convert.L <- p60$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p60.Convert.U <- p60$U[1, 2]
con.60 <- c(60, p60.Convert, p60.Convert.L, p60.Convert.U)
## 70 year olds
p70 <-
  pmatrix.msm(gh.mod.mnm, covariates = list(Age = 70), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p70.Convert <- p70$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p70.Convert.L <- p70$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p70.Convert.U <- p70$U[1, 2]
con.70 <- c(70, p70.Convert, p70.Convert.L, p70.Convert.U)
### ## 80 year olds
p80 <-
  pmatrix.msm(gh.mod.mnm, covariates = list(Age = 80), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p80.Convert <- p80$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p80.Convert.L <- p80$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p80.Convert.U <- p80$U[1, 2]
con.80 <- c(80, p80.Convert, p80.Convert.L, p80.Convert.U)
# ### ## 90 year olds
p90 <-
  pmatrix.msm(gh.mod.mnm, covariates = list(Age = 90), ci = "normal")
# # Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p90.Convert <- p90$estimates[1, 2]
# # set lower bound
p90.Convert.L <- p90$L[1, 2]
p90.Convert.U <- p90$U[1, 2]
con.90 <- c(90, p90.Convert, p90.Convert.L, p90.Convert.U)
# ### create data frame for conversion plot
conplot <-
  data.frame(rbind(con.20, con.30, con.40, con.50, con.60, con.70, con.80, con.90))
colnames(conplot) <-
  c("age", "conv_probability", "conv_lower", "conv_upper")
library(ggplot2)
#library(wesanderson)
#wes_palette("Zissou1", type = c("discrete"))
#library("ggsci")
plot.con <-
  ggplot(aes(
    x = age,
    y = conv_probability,
    ymin = conv_lower,
    ymax = conv_upper
  ),
  data = conplot) + geom_point(stroke = 2, colour = "#d1495b")  +
  geom_linerange(alpha =   1, colour = "#d1495b") +
  geom_smooth(colour = "#d1495b",
              method = "loess",
              se = FALSE)  +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, .1)) +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Religious Affiliation Transition Probability w/ 95% CI") + theme_bw() + labs(title = "Religious (Dis)affiliation Dynamics in New Zealand: years 2010 -- 2020")

#plot.con
#gh.mod.mnm

##### CREAT PLOT FOR DECONVERSION
## 20 year olds
p20 <-
  pmatrix.msm(gh.mod.mnm, covariates = list(Age = 20), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p20.decon <- p20$estimates[2, 1]
p20.decon.L <- p20$L[2, 1]
p20.decon.U <- p20$U[2, 1]
decon.20 <- c(20, p20.decon, p20.decon.L, p20.decon.U)
p30 <-
  pmatrix.msm(gh.mod.mnm, covariates = list(Age = 30), ci = "normal")
p30.decon <- p30$estimates[2, 1]
p30.decon.L <- p30$L[2, 1]
p30.decon.U <- p30$U[2, 1]
decon.30 <- c(30, p30.decon, p30.decon.L, p30.decon.U)
p40 <-
  pmatrix.msm(gh.mod.mnm, covariates = list(Age = 40), ci = "normal")
p40.decon <- p40$estimates[2, 1]
p40.decon.L <- p40$L[2, 1]
p40.decon.U <- p40$U[2, 1]
decon.40 <- c(40, p40.decon, p40.decon.L, p40.decon.U)
p50 <-
  pmatrix.msm(gh.mod.mnm, covariates = list(Age = 50), ci = "normal")
p50.decon <- p50$estimates[2, 1]
p50.decon.L <- p50$L[2, 1]
p50.decon.U <- p50$U[2, 1]
decon.50 <- c(50, p50.decon, p50.decon.L, p50.decon.U)
p60 <-
  pmatrix.msm(gh.mod.mnm, covariates = list(Age = 60), ci = "normal")
p60.decon <- p60$estimates[2, 1]
p60.decon.L <- p60$L[2, 1]
p60.decon.U <- p60$U[2, 1]
decon.60 <- c(60, p60.decon, p60.decon.L, p60.decon.U)
p70 <-
  pmatrix.msm(gh.mod.mnm, covariates = list(Age = 70), ci = "normal")
p70.decon <- p70$estimates[2, 1]
p70.decon.L <- p70$L[2, 1]
p70.decon.U <- p70$U[2, 1]
decon.70 <- c(70, p70.decon, p70.decon.L, p70.decon.U)
p80 <-
  pmatrix.msm(gh.mod.mnm, covariates = list(Age = 80), ci = "normal")
p80.decon <- p80$estimates[2, 1]
p80.decon.L <- p80$L[2, 1]
p80.decon.U <- p80$U[2, 1]
decon.80 <- c(80, p80.decon, p80.decon.L, p80.decon.U)
p90 <-
  pmatrix.msm(gh.mod.mnm, covariates = list(Age = 90), ci = "normal")
p90.decon <- p90$estimates[2, 1]
p90.decon.L <- p90$L[2, 1]
p90.decon.U <- p90$U[2, 1]
decon.90 <- c(90, p90.decon, p90.decon.L, p90.decon.U)

### create data frame for conversion plot
deconplot <-
  data.frame(rbind(
    decon.20,
    decon.30,
    decon.40,
    decon.50,
    decon.60,
    decon.70,
    decon.80,
    decon.90
  ))
colnames(deconplot) <-
  c("age", "decon_probability", "decon_lower", "decon_upper")
library(ggplot2)
plot.decon <-
  ggplot(aes(
    x = age,
    y = decon_probability,
    ymin = decon_lower,
    ymax = decon_upper
  ),
  data = deconplot) +
  geom_point(alpha = 1,
             colour = "#00798c",
             stroke = 2) +   geom_linerange(alpha = 1, colour = "#00798c") +
  #scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,.1))    +
  geom_smooth(colour = "#00798c", se = T) +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Religious Disaffiliation Transition Probability w/ 95% CI") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Religious (Dis)affiliation Dynamics")  +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

library(gridExtra)
#grid.arrange(plot.con,plot.decon, ncol=2)
### SUPERIMPOSE
deconplot$Group <- as.factor(rep("Disaffiliation", nrow(deconplot)))
conplot$Group <- as.factor(rep("Affiliation", nrow(conplot)))
deconplot2 <- deconplot %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    conv_probability = decon_probability,
    conv_lower = decon_lower,
    conv_upper = decon_upper
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-c(decon_probability, decon_upper, decon_lower))

comboplot <- rbind(conplot, deconplot2)
#comboplot
# get initial probabilities for title label
#pmatrix.msm(gh.mod.mnm)

library(ggplot2)
plot.combo <-
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = age,
      y = conv_probability,
      ymin = conv_lower,
      ymax = conv_upper,
      colour = Group
    ),
    data = comboplot
  ) +
  geom_point(alpha = 1, stroke = 2) +
  geom_linerange(alpha = 1) +
  geom_smooth(se = F) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, .1))  +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Annual Probability of Religious (Dis)affiliation w/ 95 CI: range: Pr = 0 to Pr = 1/100 ") + theme_bw() + labs(title = "Religious (Dis)affiliation Dynamics in New Zealand: years 2010 -- 2020") +
  #scale_colour_manual(values=c( "#E69F00", "#56B4E9")) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#d1495b", "#00798c")) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")


plot.combo

#pr / plot.combo + plot_annotation(title = "Religious Change over Time",
#         subtitle = "(i) Society-level aggregate change (ii) Individual-level process: affiliation vs. disaffiliation",
#tag_levels = "i")
```
